lead: This week in tech highlights significant advancements in AI applications, innovative product launches, and the evolving landscape of the Australian tech ecosystem.

stories.title: OpenAI’s existential questions
stories.summary: In a recent episode of Equity, discussions centered on OpenAI's acquisitions aimed at addressing significant challenges the company faces. The conversation delves into the implications of these moves for the future of AI and the ethical considerations surrounding its development.

stories.title: Tech expert tips Apple to launch folding smartphone
stories.summary: Tech expert Trevor Long predicts that Apple is poised to introduce a foldable smartphone this year, marking a significant shift in the company's product lineup. This potential launch could redefine user experiences and set new standards in mobile technology.

stories.title: The NSA is reportedly using Anthropic's new model Mythos
stories.summary: Despite previous tensions, the NSA has begun utilizing Anthropic's Mythos Preview, a language model designed for computer security tasks. This development highlights the growing intersection of AI and national security, raising questions about the ethical implications of such technologies.

stories.title: Carsales follows customers into AI chat apps
stories.summary: Carsales is adapting to customer preferences by integrating AI chat applications into its services. This move reflects a broader trend in the Australian tech ecosystem where companies are leveraging AI to enhance user engagement and streamline operations.

stories.title: Blue Origin successfully reused its New Glenn rocket
stories.summary: Blue Origin achieved a milestone by successfully reusing its New Glenn rocket, although the mission faced challenges with the satellite's deployment. This accomplishment underscores the importance of reusable technology in the aerospace sector.

stories.title: Humanoid robot beats human half-marathon world record
stories.summary: In a remarkable display of technological advancement, a humanoid robot named Lightning completed a half-marathon in Beijing, surpassing the human world record. This event showcases the rapid progress in robotics and AI capabilities.

stories.title: Tesla rolls out Robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
stories.summary: Tesla is expanding its Robotaxi service into Dallas and Houston, marking a significant step in its autonomous vehicle strategy. This expansion positions Tesla to compete directly with other autonomous ride-hailing services in these markets.

stories.title: Google in talks to build custom AI inference chips
stories.summary: Google is negotiating with Marvell Technology to develop new AI chips, diversifying its hardware partnerships. This initiative aims to enhance Google's capabilities in AI processing and reflects the growing demand for custom silicon in the tech industry.

stories.title: Palantir posts mini-manifesto on inclusivity and culture
stories.summary: Palantir has stirred controversy by releasing a manifesto that critiques inclusivity and certain cultural dynamics. This bold statement has sparked discussions about the company's values and its role in the tech landscape.
